destinations.evidence: The dispatcher returns defined ZK Phase 1 fees for 0x0806 using fixed executor fee, input commitment fee, and 64-byte inclusion output.

equivalence_argument: ZK_TWO_PHASE_PRECOMPILE no longer falls through to unknown-precompile handling; it is routed before generic HTTP/LLM pricing to calculate_zk_fees_phase1.
notes: This maps directly to the ZK phase-one fee branch.
rationale: The primary diff adds `ZK_PHASE1_SETTLEMENT_FEE_WEI`, `calculate_zk_fees_phase1`, and a dispatch branch in `calculate_async_fees` for `ZK_TWO_PHASE_PRECOMPILE`; the commit body says the missing branch caused payload settlement construction to fail as an unknown precompile. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: For `ZK_TWO_PHASE_PRECOMPILE` (`0x0806`), `calculate_async_fees` must return Phase 1 fees using a fixed executor fee, normal async commitment fee for the input length, and inclusion fee computed with a small 64-byte Phase 1 output size.
suggested_falsification_tests: Call `calculate_async_fees(ZK_TWO_PHASE_PRECOMPILE, input, output)` for representative input and assert it returns `AsyncFees::new(U256::from(ZK_PHASE1_SETTLEMENT_FEE_WEI), async_commitment_fee(input.len()), async_inclusion_fee(input.len(), 64))` rather than an unknown-precompile error.
summary: ZK two-phase Phase 1 has explicit settlement fees.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Call `calculate_async_fees(ZK_TWO_PHASE_PRECOMPILE, input, output)` for representative input and assert it returns `AsyncFees::new(U256::from(ZK_PHASE1_SETTLEMENT_FEE_WEI), async_commitment_fee(input.len()), async_inclusion_fee(input.len(), 64))` rather than an unknown-precompile error.
title: ZK two-phase Phase 1 has defined settlement fees
